components.name: Button
components.summary: Productivity-app button with primary/secondary/ghost/danger variants, sm/md/lg sizes, optional leading icon, disabled state. Themes via --mchat-accent and --mchat-radius-md.

contracts.constructor: new Button({label, variant?, size?, icon?, onClick?, target?, disabled?, fullWidth?})

components.name: TextInput
components.summary: Single-line text entry with placeholder, optional label, leading icon, clearable affordance, onInput live callback, and Enter-to-submit semantics. Drop-in for add-task, search, note-title.

contracts.constructor: new TextInput({value?, placeholder?, label?, leadingIcon?, clearable?, size?, type?, onInput?, onSubmit?, onBlur?, target?})
contracts.submitContract: onSubmit(value, input) fires when user presses Enter - call input.clear() inside if you want add-and-clear UX

components.name: Checkbox
components.summary: Custom-styled checkbox with native <input> for accessibility, optional label, strike-through-when-checked todo behavior toggleable. Tracks --mchat-accent for the checked fill.

contracts.constructor: new Checkbox({label?, checked?, disabled?, strikeOnCheck?, onChange?, target?})
contracts.changeContract: onChange(checked, box) fires on user toggle; programmatic setChecked(v, silent=true) suppresses it

components.name: AppShell
components.summary: Header + sidebar + main + footer skeleton for productivity apps. Responsive collapse below 720px with hamburger toggle and dimming scrim. Slots are exposed as element references for composition.

contracts.compositionRule: Mount inside <body>; let it occupy 100vh. Append your widgets into the slots, do not reparent the slots themselves.

components.name: Modal
components.summary: Dialog overlay with backdrop, ESC-to-dismiss, backdrop-click-to-dismiss, scrollable body, focus-first on open, header slot + body slot + footer slot. Sizes: sm/md/lg.

contracts.constructor: new Modal({title?, size?, dismissOnBackdrop?, dismissOnEscape?, showClose?, onOpen?, onClose?, target?})
contracts.compositionRule: Append form fields to bodySlot, action buttons to footerSlot. Call open() to show, close() to dismiss.

components.name: ListItem
components.summary: Single row in a list/sidebar: leading slot (checkbox/avatar/icon), primary + secondary text, trailing slot (badge/action). Click handler, active visual state, compact density variant.

contracts.constructor: new ListItem({primary, secondary?, leading?, trailing?, onClick?, active?, density?, multiline?, target?})
contracts.clickContract: onClick ignores events from interactive children (button/input/a) so embedded checkboxes/buttons fire their own handlers cleanly

components.name: TimerRing
components.summary: Circular SVG countdown ring with monospace time in the middle and optional caption. Self-paced setInterval ticker; pause/resume/reset. Color shifts to --mchat-success on complete.

contracts.constructor: new TimerRing({totalSeconds, remaining?, size?, thickness?, caption?, onTick?, onComplete?, target?})
contracts.tickContract: Internal interval at 250ms; onTick(remaining, ring) and onComplete(ring) fire as appropriate. For external timing, skip start() and use setRemaining().

components.name: EmptyState
components.summary: Placeholder shown when a list/grid is empty: large icon, title, subtitle, optional CTA button. Toggle show()/hide() based on item count.

contracts.constructor: new EmptyState({icon?, title, subtitle?, ctaLabel?, onCta?, target?})
contracts.compositionRule: Mount inside the list container; toggle visibility based on item count. Don't unmount/remount - keeps animations stable.

components.name: Column
components.summary: Kanban-style swimlane with header (title + count + slot), scrollable body, and optional + Add footer button. Width 280px desktop / 280px mobile-swipe.

contracts.constructor: new Column({title, accent?, count?, onAdd?, addLabel?, target?})

components.name: Card
components.summary: Visual content card: optional image (16:9), title, subtitle, body, tag chips, trailing action row. Click handler ignores nested interactive children. <article> root for semantic grouping.

contracts.constructor: new Card({title, subtitle?, body?, image?, tags?, onClick?, target?})

components.name: TagChip
components.summary: Small colored pill for tags, categories, statuses. Variants: success/warning/danger/neutral. Optional custom color, optional dismissible x button, optional click handler.

contracts.constructor: new TagChip({label, color?, variant?, showDot?, dismissible?, onClick?, onDismiss?, target?})

components.name: DataTable
components.summary: Sortable tabular display. Column config (key, label, width, align, sortable, render?), row click handler, empty state. Tabular-numeric alignment on right-aligned columns. Sticky header.

contracts.constructor: new DataTable({columns:[{key,label,width?,align?,sortable?,render?}], rows:[{key:value}], onRowClick?, emptyText?, initialSort?, target?})
contracts.renderContract: column.render(value, row) -> string or DOM Node. Use for currency, dates, badges, action buttons.

components.name: DonutChart
components.summary: Categorical breakdown SVG donut with center label/subtitle and optional legend. Auto-palette if colors not specified per slice. Pure SVG, no chart libs needed.

contracts.constructor: new DonutChart({data:[{label, value, color?}], centerLabel?, centerSubtitle?, size?, thickness?, showLegend?, valueFormat?, onSliceClick?, target?})
contracts.valueFormat: Function (value) => string for legend + slice tooltips. Default String(value).

components.name: LevelProgression
components.summary: N-level progression manager with cumulative score tracking across cleared levels. Mounts a hidden [data-mchatai-score] DOM element so the hosted-page leaderboard bridge reads the full run (sum of cleared levels + partial current), not the per-level reset value. Default ships 12 levels with a smooth difficulty curve; pass { levels: [...] } to override. Exposes onLevelChange + onCumulativeChange callbacks for HUD wiring. Plain JS class, NOT a custom element.

components.name: ContentDictionary
components.summary: Generic content bank for content-DRIVEN games (trivia/quiz, crossword/anagram clue->word, flashcards, riddles, guess-the-X, geography). Items are { prompt, answer, hints?, category?, difficulty?, tags?, aliases? }. draw(filter) pulls a non-repeating item (seeded deterministic order for daily-puzzle / canary replay), check(item, guess) validates case-insensitively against answer + aliases, hint(item, n) reveals progressively. Sibling of entities.word-dictionary (which is for word-TRACE membership/prefix games). Plain JS class, NOT a custom element.

components.name: LevelContentData
components.summary: Data-driven level definitions + difficulty scaling + save/load. The CONTENT half of multi-level games (entities.level-progression is the runtime STATE half; ui.level-select-screen is the picker). Levels are { targetScore?, moveBudget?, timeLimit?, difficulty?, params?, rewards?, unlocks? }; params is the scaled gameplay knob bag (enemySpeed, spawnRate, gridSize) so games read level.params.X instead of hardcoding per-level branches. generate({ count, baseTarget, scale }) builds a smooth curve (honors wisdom lb-016, 8+ levels). toProgressionLevels() projects to the { target, moves } shape LevelProgression consumes. Persists furthest-unlocked + per-level best to localStorage for continue-from-level. Plain JS class.

helpers: LevelContentData.generate({ count, baseTarget, targetGrowth?, scale?, baseMoves? })

components.name: LevelSelectScreen
components.summary: Responsive level-picker grid for multi-level / campaign games. Renders a card per level: unlocked = tappable, locked = dimmed with a lock glyph, plus a difficulty badge, level name, and the player's best score. Reads unlock + best-score state from a LevelContentData instance; fires onSelect(index, level) on tap. Mobile-first (>=88px tap targets, keyboard accessible), uses canonical --mchat-* swatch tokens with inline fallbacks (FOUC-safe). show()/hide()/refresh(). Plain JS class, NOT a custom element.

contracts.factory: new LevelSelectScreen({ host, data, title?, onSelect? })

components.name: CrosswordGenerator
components.summary: Deterministic crossword GENERATOR. Give it a bank of { answer, clue } items (resources.crossword-dictionary, 400+ words); it assembles a real interlocking mini/midi crossword: solution grid (letters + '#'), clue numbering, across/down lists. Seeded (no Math.random) for daily puzzles + replay. PLAIN JS CLASS; pair with ui.crossword-board.

build: generate(items) -> {grid, numbers, across, down, rows, cols, wordCount}

components.name: CrosswordBoard
components.summary: Renders + plays a generated crossword (from entities.crossword-generator). Give it a root + a puzzle; it builds the numbered grid, across/down clues, active-word highlight, keyboard + click entry, across/down toggle, Check, timer, solved state. PLAIN JS CLASS: new CrosswordBoard({root,puzzle,onSolved}).start().

components.name: CrosswordDictionary
components.summary: Deep crossword word/clue bank: 425 { answer (A-Z), clue, difficulty, category } items weighted to 3-5 letters for dense interlock. Feed CROSSWORD_BANK to entities.crossword-generator. Every answer is a real word, so every slot is real (wisdom pg-016); far exceeds pg-017's >=100. DATA module, no logic.

components.name: SpanishPhrasebook
components.summary: Deep Spanish<->English travel phrasebook: 421 { es (accented Spanish), en (ASCII English), category } phrases across greetings, dining, directions, hotel, shopping, transport, emergencies, time/numbers. Feed SPANISH_PHRASES to a language memory-match (pair es<->en via MemoryMatchBoard.buildPairDeck) or flashcards via entities.content-dictionary. DATA module.

compositionRecipes.name: Add Levels / Campaign (addon)
assemblyNotes: This is an ADDON recipe. LAYERS ON TOP of the base game recipe - never replaces it. The marker MUST be `{recipe: <base-recipe-id>, addons: ["recipe.with-level-progression"], components: [...all base + addon components...]}`. This is the canonical answer to 'add levels' / 'make it a campaign' / 'add harder stages' on an existing single-screen game.

IMPORT SHAPE - named exports only: `import { LevelContentData } from "./web-components/entities/LevelContentData.js";` `import { LevelProgression } from "./web-components/entities/LevelProgression.js";` `import { LevelSelectScreen } from "./web-components/ui/LevelSelectScreen.js";`.

DATA, STATE, PICKER - keep the three roles separate. LevelContentData is the campaign DATA (use `LevelContentData.generate({ count, baseTarget, scale: { <gameKnob>: [start, end] } })` so difficulty scales smoothly instead of hardcoding per-level numbers; read scaled knobs via `level.params.<knob>` in the game loop). LevelProgression is the runtime STATE during a level (`new LevelProgression({ levels: data.toProgressionLevels() })`). LevelSelectScreen is the PICKER (`new LevelSelectScreen({ host, data, onSelect: (i) => startLevel(data.level(i)) })`).

SAVE/LOAD - LevelContentData persists furthest-unlocked + per-level best to localStorage. On level clear call `data.recordClear(i, score)`; the picker's `refresh()` then shows the next level unlocked and the new best. Pass `{ storageKey: '<game>-progress' }` to namespace per game.

WISDOM lb-016 - ship 8+ levels (or endless). `LevelContentData.generate` warns under 8. For endless games, generate a long curve and loop the last level's params with an explicit 'Endless' label rather than auto-restarting at level 1.
DO NOT double-implement - if the base game already has its own level/stage system, STOP and report 'already-staged' instead of layering a second progression.

compositionRecipes.name: Open-World Vector Blast
compositionRecipes.cloneTarget: Vector-style space shooter expanded into a camera-following open world
assemblyNotes: Use CameraFollow and world coordinates instead of wraparound screen coordinates.
Use Turret for moon/base installations and Projectile for both player and enemy fire.
Keep Asteroid splitting from the normal Asteroids recipe, but spawn/despawn objects around the camera viewport.

compositionRecipes.name: Vector Blast / Top-Down Arcade
compositionRecipes.cloneTarget: Vector-style top-down arcade shooter with screen-wrap and inertia
assemblyNotes: Write glue code around the shipped engine and entity modules instead of rewriting movement, wrapping, projectile, and scoring logic from scratch.
For open-world Asteroids variants, keep SpaceShip and Projectile but disable world wrapping and add camera/world coordinates in the assembly layer.

compositionRecipes.name: Defender Clone
compositionRecipes.cloneTarget: Defender-style horizontal scrolling rescue shooter
assemblyNotes: Build a recognizable Defender clone: horizontal wrap or long-scrolling world, ship always free-flying, enemy abductors, rescue targets/humans, radar/minimap, lives, score, and escalating waves.

Use GameManager, Physics2D, Projectile, CameraFollow, ScoreBoard, Swatches, and AudioManager as the baseplate. Only generate the game-specific enemy wave, radar, terrain, and rescue glue until dedicated Defender blocks are promoted.
Instantiate GameManager with an options object like new GameManager({ canvas, width, height, onUpdate, onDraw }); never call new GameManager(ctx).

compositionRecipes.name: Paddle Bounce
compositionRecipes.cloneTarget: Two-paddle ball bounce arcade game
assemblyNotes: Use Paddle and Ball as fixed rules primitives; only generate scoring rules, input binding, and theme.
Use GameManager callbacks (`onUpdate`, `onDraw`) and `game.start()`; there is no GameManager.update() API.
Use PongAI to drive the CPU paddle. Without delay+error parameters a perfect ball-tracker is unbeatable; defaults (110ms / 12%) make it fun.

compositionRecipes.name: Lane Hopper
compositionRecipes.cloneTarget: Lane-by-lane road and river crossing arcade game
assemblyNotes: Use GridBoard for lanes, GridMover for the player, and PathFollower for cars/logs; generate only lane data and collision rules.

compositionRecipes.name: Chomp-Man
compositionRecipes.cloneTarget: Maze-chase arcade game with pellets, power dots, and roaming enemies
assemblyNotes: Use GridBoard for maze walls and pellets, GridMover for player/ghost movement, and AIPathfinder for ghost chase/scatter behavior.
Instantiate documented component constructors directly; there is no framework-level initialize() API.

compositionRecipes.name: Pixel Side-Scrolling Platformer
compositionRecipes.cloneTarget: Side-scrolling platformer or isometric hopper arcade
assemblyNotes: Build a recognizable side-scrolling platformer clone: player, platforms, gaps, coins, hazards, lives, score, camera follow, keyboard/touch controls, and restart.

Render the player with PLUMBER (or KNIGHT for adventure-flavored), coins with COIN_FRAMES animation, pipes with PIPE, bricks with BRICK, enemies with GOOMBA - all from resources/SpritePresets.js. Plain colored rectangles for player/enemies are forbidden (vq-001).

For Pyramid Hopper variants, use a hex-staircase background pattern + KNIGHT player + GOOMBA enemies. The platformer recipe is the right home for this - there is no separate `recipe.pyramid-hopper` (yet).
Use PlatformerPlayer for movement/jumping and CameraFollow for side scrolling; do not omit the mchatai-web-components-used marker.

compositionRecipes.name: Tower Defense Clone
compositionRecipes.cloneTarget: Desktop Tower Defense / Bloons-style path defense game
assemblyNotes: Build a recognizable tower defense clone: visible path, build pads, turrets, enemy waves, projectiles, lives, gold, score, and Start Wave control.

Use TurretVariants - BasicTurret / CannonTurret / FrostTurret / BeamTurret - to give the player real build choices. A single turret type is boring; the four variants have distinct stats (cost, range, cooldown, damage, splash, slow) so players plan placement.
Render each turret with its bundled sprite (TURRET_BASIC, TURRET_CANNON, TURRET_FROST, TURRET_BEAM from SpritePresets). Do NOT render turrets as plain circles - wisdom rule vq-001 forbids it.
Add a small turret-shop UI with one button per variant showing cost; selecting a variant before clicking a build pad determines what gets placed.
Instantiate documented component constructors directly; there is no framework-level initialize() API.

compositionRecipes.name: Word Quest
compositionRecipes.cloneTarget: Five-letter word guessing puzzle with six guesses, colored feedback (correct, wrong-spot, missing), and an on-screen QWERTY keyboard
assemblyNotes: Build Word Quest as a Wordle clone, not a generic word builder: one hidden five-letter answer, six rows of five cells, Enter/Delete/New Word controls, green/yellow/gray feedback, and colored QWERTY keyboard keys.
Use LetterGrid for guesses and feedback; generate dictionary/level content separately from grid scoring.

Adopt effects/ Legos for visual feedback: BoardShake.shake(row) on invalid guesses, ScoreRise.show(row, '+points STREAK') on correct. Each effect Lego exports an injectCss() helper - call once on init so @keyframes are present before the first event. A wordle without invalid-shake or score-rise reads as broken even when logic is correct.

compositionRecipes.name: Squaredle / Bookworm Letter-Trace
assemblyNotes: NOTE 2026-05-09: core.game-manager DELIBERATELY OMITTED from this recipe. The entity Lego (e.g. entities.word-trace-grid) is a self-contained state machine - instantiate directly with `new`, drive your own setInterval/requestAnimationFrame loop. See wisdom rule fs-015.

Use WordTraceGrid for the 4x4 (or NxN) board. Listen to pointerdown/pointermove/pointerup with `passive: false` and call grid.startTrace / extendTrace / commitWord. (See mobile-frontend wisdom mob-005.)
Bundle a 2-10k word dictionary inline as a JS array; pass it to WordDictionary. For tests, use the exported COMMON_WORDS_SAMPLE (~1000 words).
Use WordDictionary.findAllOnGrid(grid) at puzzle generation to compute the FULL set of legal words for that letter arrangement. Use the count + total possible score as the level target.
Highlight adjacent cells visually. Use isPrefix(currentWord) during pointermove to color-cue the user when their trace can still complete a valid word.
Mobile-portrait full-bleed. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules (mob-001..016). Touch targets >= 44x44 logical pixels.
Persist foundWords + best score in localStorage. Show 'X / Y words found' progress. End-of-puzzle bonus screen lists missed words via WordDictionary.findAllOnGrid.

compositionRecipes.name: Code Breaker
compositionRecipes.cloneTarget: Color-peg code breaker with exact-position and color-only feedback
assemblyNotes: Use MastermindLogic for code generation and feedback pegs; do not ask the model to reinvent exact/color-only scoring.
Use ScoreBoard.setScore(), ScoreBoard.add(), or ScoreBoard.reset(); there is no ScoreBoard.update() API.
On win OR turns-exhausted, call RestartOverlay.show() with a clear title (e.g. 'Code cracked!' / 'Code locked'). Wire onRestart to newGame().
On win, submit the final score via Leaderboard.submitFinal(score, { result, turns, codeLength }) and renderRankCard against a #rankCard host.

compositionRecipes.name: Minesweeper Clone
compositionRecipes.cloneTarget: Minesweeper-style grid reveal puzzle
assemblyNotes: Use RevealLogic for mine seeding, adjacency counts, flags, and flood reveal; generate board size/difficulty settings only.
On win OR mine-hit, call RestartOverlay.show() with title/subtitle. On win, submit final score via Leaderboard.submitFinal(score, { result, time_seconds, mines, rows, cols }).

compositionRecipes.name: Match-Three Clone
compositionRecipes.cloneTarget: Bejeweled / Candy Crush-style swap-and-cascade match-three puzzle
assemblyNotes: Use TileSwapper for selection/swap input and Match3Logic for matches/cascades; generate tile skins and level goals only.
Do not stop at an import shell. A valid match-three app must render a visible tile board, selectable adjacent swaps, match/cascade resolution, score/combo HUD, and restart/shuffle controls.

Adopt the effects/ Legos for visual feedback: SpawnDrop for tile entry/exit, PopBurst on every match, ScoreRise on score events, BoardShake on invalid swaps, HintPulse for idle hints. Each effect exports an injectCss() helper - call once on init so the @keyframes are present. A board with no animations on match/swap/score events should be treated as missing required juice, not 'minimal-by-design'.

Use LevelProgression to manage 8-12 levels with cumulative score. Do not reset the leaderboard score per level: LevelProgression.mount() injects a hidden [data-mchatai-score] element that the hosted-page bridge reads, so submitted scores reflect the entire run. After the final level, show a New Game+ button instead of looping back to level 1.

compositionRecipes.name: 2048 / Threes Swipe-Merge
assemblyNotes: NOTE 2026-05-09: core.game-manager DELIBERATELY OMITTED from this recipe. The entity Lego (e.g. entities.swipe-tile-grid) is a self-contained state machine - instantiate directly with `new`, drive your own setInterval/requestAnimationFrame loop. See wisdom rule fs-015.
Use SwipeTileGrid for the 4x4 tile grid (size: 4 default, or pass size: 5/6 for harder variants).

Mobile-first: detect swipe via pointerdown + pointerup, NOT keyboard. Use SwipeTileGrid.directionFromSwipe(startX, startY, endX, endY, 30) to convert deltas to direction. (See mobile-frontend wisdom mob-005.)
Desktop fallback: also bind ArrowUp/ArrowDown/ArrowLeft/ArrowRight to grid.swipe(direction).
After every successful swipe (result.moved == true), call grid.spawnRandomTile() to add a new 2 or 4. Track grid.score for the scoreboard.
Use effects.spawn-drop for new-tile entrance animation and effects.score-rise for merge feedback (per merge points).
Color palette: tiles get distinct colors per power. Use a logarithmic gradient (2 = lightest -> 2048+ = boldest accent). Pull from resources.swatches.
End-of-game: when grid.isGameOver(), show overlay with final score + best tile + 'Try Again' button (mac-022 web equivalent - visible-restart).
On grid.isWin() === true (first time the win-value tile appears), show a 'You won! Keep playing?' overlay; let the user continue past 2048.
Persist best score + best tile in localStorage. Show end-of-game vs personal-best comparison ONLY at game-over, not in-play (per high-score-is-end-of-run-chrome wisdom).
Mobile-portrait full-bleed. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules (mob-001..016). Touch targets >= 44x44 logical pixels.

compositionRecipes.name: Memory Match / Concentration
assemblyNotes: NOTE 2026-05-09: core.game-manager DELIBERATELY OMITTED from this recipe. The entity Lego (e.g. entities.memory-match-board) is a self-contained state machine - instantiate directly with `new`, drive your own setInterval/requestAnimationFrame loop. See wisdom rule fs-015.
Use MemoryMatchBoard for the card grid. Pick 4x4 (16 cards / 8 pairs) for kids/easy, 4x6 (24 / 12 pairs) for medium, 6x6 (36 / 18 pairs) for hard.

Call MemoryMatchBoard.buildShuffledDeck(symbols, rows * cols) once per new game. MEMORY_DEFAULT_SYMBOLS has 36 emoji; substitute with country flags / animal photos / chess-piece glyphs / etc. for thematic variants.

Touch UX (mob-005): listen to pointerup on each card element, NOT click. Call grid.flipCard(r, c). The 'state' return value tells you what to do: 'first' (just flip); 'match' (apply matched class to both, play match SFX); 'mismatch' (apply face-up briefly, then setTimeout(800ms) -> grid.resolveMismatch() -> re-render); 'ignored' (no-op).
Animate card flips with CSS transform: rotateY(180deg) and a face-back / face-front structure. Touch targets >= 44x44 logical pixels (mob-002).
Score model: track grid.moves (pair-attempts) and grid.misses. Final score = (totalPairs / moves) * 1000 OR a time-based scheme. Show end-of-game stats: pairs found / moves / misses / time elapsed.
End-of-game overlay (mac-022 web equivalent): grid.isComplete() -> show 'Try Again' button + 'New Theme' button (lets user pick a different symbol set).
Persist best-moves and best-time in localStorage per board size.
Mobile-portrait full-bleed. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules (mob-001..016).

compositionRecipes.name: Sudoku 9x9 Logic Puzzle
assemblyNotes: NOTE 2026-05-09: core.game-manager DELIBERATELY OMITTED from this recipe. The entity Lego (e.g. entities.sudoku-board) is a self-contained state machine - instantiate directly with `new`, drive your own setInterval/requestAnimationFrame loop. See wisdom rule fs-015.
Use SudokuBoard for the 9x9 grid. Generate puzzles via SudokuBoard.generatePuzzle('easy'|'medium'|'hard'|'expert') - caller doesn't need a hard-coded puzzle list.
Render with 3x3 subgrid borders (heavier 2px lines every 3 rows/cols). Locked clue cells get distinct styling (bolder font + slightly darker bg).

Touch UX (mob-005): pointerup to select a cell; show a 1-9 number pad below the grid (touch targets >= 44x44 per mob-002). Caller maps tap of a number -> grid.setCell(activeR, activeC, value); validate immediately and highlight conflicts via grid.validateCell.
Pencil-mark mode: a toggle button switches input from 'commit value' to 'toggle note'. Notes display as small 1-9 candidates inside the cell.

Highlight features (huge UX boost): when a cell is selected, soft-highlight (a) same row, (b) same column, (c) same 3x3 subgrid, (d) all cells with the same value. Conflict cells get red highlight via allConflicts().
Win detection: after every setCell, if grid.isComplete() show overlay with elapsed time + difficulty + 'New Puzzle' button (mac-022 web equivalent - visible-restart).
Persist current puzzle in localStorage so refresh doesn't lose progress. Save: JSON.stringify(grid.cells).
Mobile-portrait full-bleed. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules (mob-001..016).
Difficulty selector (segmented control or pills): easy/medium/hard/expert. Each picks a different generatePuzzle clue count.

compositionRecipes.name: Hangman Word-Guess
assemblyNotes: NOTE 2026-05-09: core.game-manager DELIBERATELY OMITTED from this recipe. The entity Lego (e.g. entities.hangman-game) is a self-contained state machine - instantiate directly with `new`, drive your own setInterval/requestAnimationFrame loop. See wisdom rule fs-015.
Use HangmanGame for state. Pick a fresh word from HANGMAN_DEFAULT_WORDS (or a thematic substitute) on each round. Caller-controlled vocabulary => easy to swap in animals / movies / foods / countries.

Render 3 surfaces: (1) the gallows + hangman drawing (SVG with paths revealed by wrongCount: head=1, body=2, lArm=3, rArm=4, lLeg=5, rLeg=6); (2) the masked word as widely-spaced underscores/letters; (3) an A-Z on-screen keyboard.
On-screen keyboard touch UX (mob-005): pointerup per key. Disable already-guessed letters (or grey them out + apply correct/incorrect color). Use grid.remainingLetters() to drive enabled state.
Color cues: correct guesses = green letter on the keyboard; incorrect = red. Animate the masked word's letter when it gets revealed.
End-of-round overlay (mac-022 web equivalent): if state==='won' show 'You won!'; if state==='lost' show 'Game over' + the word that was hidden. Both overlays have a 'New Word' button.
Persist streak / longest streak in localStorage. Show streak in the header.
Touch targets >= 44x44 logical pixels (mob-002). Mobile-portrait full-bleed. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules (mob-001..016).

compositionRecipes.name: Block Stack Falling Blocks
keywords: I O T L J S Z
assemblyNotes: NOTE 2026-05-09: core.game-manager DELIBERATELY OMITTED from this recipe. The entity Lego (e.g. entities.tetris-board) is a self-contained state machine - instantiate directly with `new`, drive your own setInterval/requestAnimationFrame loop. See wisdom rule fs-015.
Use TetrisBoard for the 10x20 grid (or pass rows: 22 / cols: 10 for the standard 'extra rows above' rendering).
Game loop: setInterval(() => grid.tick(), grid.gravityMs()) - re-call setInterval whenever lines clear (level changes -> faster gravity).

Mobile touch input (mob-005): pointerdown to detect tap, pointerup with delta to detect swipe (left/right = move, down = soft drop, up = rotate, swipe-up-fast = hard drop). Bottom on-screen control bar with arrow buttons + rotate as a fallback.
Desktop keyboard: ArrowLeft/Right = move, ArrowDown = softDrop, ArrowUp = rotate, Space = hardDrop, P = pause.
Use TetrisBoard.ghostPosition() to render the 'landing preview' (semi-transparent piece silhouette where the current piece would land). Standard mobile-Tetris UX feature.
Render the 'next' piece in a side-panel using TetrisBoard.TETROMINOES[grid.nextKind].rotations[0].

Scoring is built-in (100/300/500/800 x level). Level rises every 10 lines automatically. Use effects.score-rise for combo / line-clear visual feedback. Use effects.board-shake for tetris (4-line) clears.
End-of-game overlay (mac-022 web equivalent) when grid.gameOver: show final score + lines + level + 'New Game' button.
Persist best score in localStorage. Show in header.
Mobile-portrait full-bleed. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules (mob-001..016). Touch targets >= 44x44 logical pixels.

compositionRecipes.name: Klondike Solitaire
assemblyNotes: NOTE 2026-05-09: core.game-manager DELIBERATELY OMITTED from this recipe. The entity Lego (e.g. entities.solitaire-klondike) is a self-contained state machine - instantiate directly with `new`, drive your own setInterval/requestAnimationFrame loop. See wisdom rule fs-015.

Use SolitaireKlondike for ALL game state and rules. Drag-and-drop UX is the standard solitaire interaction model - listen to pointerdown on a card, pointermove to drag, pointerup to drop on a tableau column or foundation. The state machine handles all rule validation; UI just calls grid.moveFromWaste/Tableau/Foundation and reads the {ok, reason} return.
TAP-TO-AUTO-MOVE for mobile: a single tap on a card should attempt to move it to its foundation if legal (saves the user a long drag). Use canStackFoundation to check.
Render face-down cards as a card-back pattern (gradient or pattern). Face-up cards show rank+suit using CARD_SYMBOLS.
Tableau layout: stack cards with vertical offset (~25-30px on mobile) so multiple cards in a column are visible. Top card is fully exposed.
Foundation cells start empty; show a faint suit-symbol watermark per pile so the player knows which is which.
Stock + waste in the top-right (or top-left for mobile-portrait). Drawing 3 cards: visually fan the top 3 of the waste so the player can see all 3.
Auto-complete button (effects.score-rise feedback): when no face-down cards remain in the tableau, an 'Auto Finish' button calls grid.autoFinishToFoundations() to play out the win.
End-game overlay (mac-022 web equivalent) when grid.isWon(): show 'You Won!' + final score + moves + 'New Game' button.
Persist best score in localStorage. Show in header.
Mobile-portrait full-bleed. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules (mob-001..016). Touch targets >= 44x44 logical pixels (whole card surface, not just the visible portion).

compositionRecipes.name: Classic Snake
assemblyNotes: NOTE 2026-05-09: core.game-manager DELIBERATELY OMITTED from this recipe. The entity Lego (e.g. entities.snake-game) is a self-contained state machine - instantiate directly with `new`, drive your own setInterval/requestAnimationFrame loop. See wisdom rule fs-015.
Use SnakeGame for the grid + state machine. Default 20x20 grid is standard mobile-snake size; use size:15 for kids/easy or size:25 for hard.
Game loop: setInterval(() => grid.tick(), grid.tickMs()) - re-arm setInterval whenever score changes (speed scales with score).
Mobile touch input (mob-005): swipe gestures via pointerdown + pointerup. Use SnakeGame.directionFromSwipe(sx,sy,ex,ey,30) to convert deltas to direction. Call grid.setDirection(dir).
Desktop keyboard: ArrowKeys + WASD. Bind keydown to grid.setDirection.
Render via canvas (faster than DOM for 20x20 cells x 60fps) OR a CSS grid with cell colors per cellValue (0=bg, 1=snake-body, 2=snake-head-bright, 3=food-accent).
Wrap mode (options.wrap=true) is a kid-friendly variant - edges wrap toroidally instead of game-over. Add a difficulty toggle.
End-game overlay (mac-022 web equivalent) on grid.gameOver: show final score + length + 'Try Again' button. Persist best score in localStorage.
effects.score-rise on each food eaten; effects.pop-burst on the food cell when consumed (small particle burst).
Mobile-portrait full-bleed. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules (mob-001..016).

compositionRecipes.name: Tappy Wings Tap-to-Flap
assemblyNotes: NOTE 2026-05-09: core.game-manager DELIBERATELY OMITTED from this recipe. The entity Lego (e.g. entities.flappy-bird) is a self-contained state machine - instantiate directly with `new`, drive your own setInterval/requestAnimationFrame loop. See wisdom rule fs-015.
Use FlappyBird for the physics + state machine. Set width/height to match the canvas/viewport. For mobile portrait: width=360, height=640 is standard.
Game loop: requestAnimationFrame. Track lastTimestamp; on each frame compute dt=(now-last)/1000, call grid.tick(dt), then render.
Mobile touch input (mob-005): pointerdown anywhere on the canvas -> grid.flap(). NO drag, NO complex gestures - flappy is tap-only.
Desktop: Space or Up-Arrow -> grid.flap(). Same handler.

Render order: background sky gradient -> pipes (top + bottom rectangles, with cap shapes for visual flair) -> bird (rotated based on vy: nose-up when going up, nose-down when falling) -> ground -> score (large, top-center).
Bird animation: bobs subtly during gameplay; rotation eases toward Math.atan2(vy, 200) clamped to [-60-degree, 90-degree] degrees.
End-game overlay (mac-022 web equivalent) on grid.isGameOver(): show final score + best score + 'Tap to play again' button.
Persist best score in localStorage. Show in header ('Best: N').
First-time UX: show a 'Tap to start' message before the first flap; the bird hovers in place until first tap.
Mobile-portrait full-bleed. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules (mob-001..016).

compositionRecipes.name: Blackjack Clone
compositionRecipes.cloneTarget: Blackjack / Twenty-One card game
assemblyNotes: Use BlackjackRules for hand totals, dealer policy, blackjack checks, and settlement; generate table flow and UI only.
Use ScoreBoard.setScore(), ScoreBoard.add(), or ScoreBoard.reset(); there is no ScoreBoard.update() API.

compositionRecipes.name: Texas Hold'em Poker Clone
compositionRecipes.cloneTarget: Texas Hold'em poker game
assemblyNotes: Layout: 4-seat oval poker table. YOU at the bottom (face-up hole cards, large stack display), then 3 CPU seats at left (West), top (North), right (East) with face-down hole cards. Community-card row across the center. Pot total beside the community cards. Per-seat current bet chips shown next to each seat.

Use core.turn-based-manager to drive the betting rounds as named phases: pre-deal -> blinds -> preflop -> flop -> turn -> river -> showdown -> next-hand. Each phase advances when all active (non-folded) players have acted.

Deck and cards: entities.deck for the 52-card shuffled deck, entities.card for individual card data (suit, rank, faceUp/faceDown). Render face-up cards with the canonical pip/suit layout. Face-down cards show the catalog back-art.

Hand evaluation: use entities.poker-hand-evaluator to compare 7-card hands (2 hole + 5 community) at showdown. The evaluator returns a rank class (High Card / Pair / Two Pair / Trips / Straight / Flush / Full House / Quads / Straight Flush) plus a tiebreaker tuple. Pick the highest-scoring 5-card combination from each player's 7 cards.

Betting actions per player turn: Check (no chips at stake to call), Call (match current bet), Bet/Raise (increase, with min-raise = previous raise amount), Fold (remove from hand). Show legal actions as enabled buttons; gray out illegal ones.

Blinds: small blind (left of dealer) and big blind (two left of dealer). Rotate dealer button clockwise each hand. Pre-flop action starts under the gun (left of big blind); post-flop starts at first remaining player left of dealer.

Pot management: track pot total + per-player current-round contribution. When a player goes all-in for less than the current bet, create side pots (one main pot capped at the all-in amount x callers, plus side pots for the remaining callers).

Stack and bankroll: each player starts with a fixed stack (e.g., $1000). Per-hand contributions deduct from stack; pot wins add back at showdown. If a player's stack reaches 0, they are eliminated; game continues with the rest.

CPU strategy: simple-but-not-stupid heuristic - fold weak hands pre-flop, call/check medium hands, raise strong hands (pair of jacks+ pre-flop, trips+ post-flop). Vary aggressiveness slightly between the 3 CPU seats so they don't behave identically (one tight, one loose, one balanced).

Hand history: scroll log showing each action and showdown reveal (e.g., 'East raises to $40', 'You call', 'Showdown: East shows AK / two pair Kings and 9s wins $140'). Limit to last 12 actions to fit the table layout.

Visual style: green felt table background (var(--mchat-accent) hue or a dark green), brown/wood rim, large card faces, chip-stack icons next to the bet amount per seat. Active player has a highlighted seat outline.
Restart: 'New Hand' button visible at all times (deals a new hand at any phase if user wants to skip). 'New Game' resets all stacks to starting value.

compositionRecipes.name: Cribbage Card Game
compositionRecipes.cloneTarget: 2-player Cribbage with pegging and show scoring
compositionRecipes.promptName: Cribbage clone (You vs CPU, peg to 121)
assemblyNotes: Cribbage rules inline: hand-deal (6 each, discard 2 to crib), pegging phase (count to 31 scoring 15s/pairs/runs/go), show phase scoring (15s/pairs/runs/flushes/his-nobs).
The crib alternates per hand - non-dealer's discards belong to dealer this hand, dealer alternates next hand.
First to 121 pegs wins. Track player+CPU pegs in ScoreBoard.
Display: both hands face-up at show phase; CPU's discards into crib stay hidden until show.
See examples/cribbage/ for a reference assembly using these Lego pieces with inline rules.
Use a visible peg board on the side (2 tracks, 121 holes each); animate peg advance as scoring happens.

compositionRecipes.name: Spades Card Game
compositionRecipes.cloneTarget: 4-player partnership Spades to 500
compositionRecipes.promptName: Spades clone (You + 3 CPU partners, bid + trick-take)
assemblyNotes: Spades is a 4-player partnership trick-taking game (You+North vs East+West).
Each hand: deal 13 cards each, each player bids 0-13 expected tricks (nil = 0 with special bonus/penalty).
Play 13 tricks following suit when possible; spades always trump.
Scoring per partnership: bidsMet => +10*bid + 1*overtricks; bidMissed => -10*bid; nilSuccess +100, nilFailure -100. Spade bag penalty optional.
Game to 500 (or first partnership to reach it).
Display: your 13 cards along bottom, current trick in center (4 cards labeled by seat), bid pills, partnership scores, who-leads marker.
See examples/spades/ for a reference using TurnBasedManager + Deck/Card with inline rules.

CARD SIZING (REQUIRED): user-hand cards must be 100-110px wide x 140-156px tall on desktop, 72-78px x 100-110px on mobile (640px width breakpoint). Opponent face-down backs tiny (28-40px wide) so they never dominate. Trick-center cards medium (78x110px desktop, 56x78px mobile). User feedback 2026-05-15: tiny 48x68px cards are unreadable; the user's own hand at the bottom is the focal point and must be clearly visible.
DO NOT include Hearts mechanics in Spades: no Pass Left phase, no 2-of-Clubs first lead, no Queen-of-Spades penalty, no Hearts Broken flag. The trump suit is SPADES; the broken flag is SPADES BROKEN.

compositionRecipes.name: Checkers Clone
compositionRecipes.cloneTarget: Checkers / Draughts board game
assemblyNotes: Use CheckersRules for diagonal movement, captures, and kinging; generate board UI and turn prompts only.
Wire CheckersAI to play one color (default: 'black'). After every human move, schedule the AI's reply via setTimeout (~350ms for thinking-feel). Never let the user play both sides.
Do not output markerless board-game HTML. Render a visible 8x8 board, selectable pieces, legal move highlights, capture feedback, turn HUD, and reset control.

compositionRecipes.name: Chess Clone
compositionRecipes.cloneTarget: standard playable Chess board game
assemblyNotes: Use ChessRules for standard setup, legal move generation, check filtering, checkmate, and stalemate.
Wire ChessAI to play one color (default: 'black'). After every human move, schedule the AI's reply via setTimeout (~350ms for thinking-feel). Never let the user play both sides.
ChessAI default depth is 2 (move under ~1s on typical hardware); raise to 3 for stronger play if you can afford up to ~3s thinking time.
Do not invent lifecycle APIs such as initialize(). Render a visible 8x8 board, selectable pieces, legal move highlights, turn/status HUD, and reset control.

compositionRecipes.name: Tic-Tac-Toe vs AI
compositionRecipes.cloneTarget: Tic-Tac-Toe / Noughts and Crosses with single-player AI
assemblyNotes: Tic-tac-toe is the H3.1 acceptance test for the H2.7 universal lifts. Wire RestartOverlay, Leaderboard, and CloudAIToggle from the start - they're inexpensive on a 3x3 game and prove the contract for harder games.

Use entities.tic-tac-toe-rules for the rules + AI. It exports TicTacToeRules, TicTacToeAI (perfect-play depth-9 minimax inline), and emptyBoard(). Do NOT roll your own rules engine - the catalog component is depth-9-perfect and battle-tested in the example.
Don't use entities.minimax-ai here - TicTacToeAI is already optimal at depth 9 and self-contained. MinimaxAI is for larger search trees (chess, checkers).
After human move: ai.pickMove(board, 'o') -> rules.applyMove(board, move, 'o') -> rules.winner(board) -> if game-over, RestartOverlay.show() + Leaderboard.submitFinal().
Score deltas: +100 for perfect-difficulty win, +50 for easy win, +25 for draw. Loss = 0 (don't submit). Adjust if you want stricter ranking math.
Inline 3x3 board as 9 buttons (CSS grid 3x3). Click handler dispatches to game.handleHumanMove(index). Easier than a generic GridBoard for this size.

compositionRecipes.name: Piano Keyboard Clone
compositionRecipes.cloneTarget: playable piano keyboard / synth toy
assemblyNotes: Build a recognizable compact piano clone: visible piano keys, click/tap play, keyboard shortcuts, current note, note trail, and synth feedback.
Use PianoKeyboard for DOM keys and KeyboardInput for hardware shortcuts; do not output an import-only shell.

compositionRecipes.name: NYT Mini Crossword Clone
compositionRecipes.cloneTarget: NYT Mini-style compact crossword puzzle with across/down clues
assemblyNotes: THIS IS A THIN ASSEMBLY (~40 lines of glue), NOT a from-scratch build. Do NOT create a bank of full puzzles (no 'dictionary of 100 puzzles'), and do NOT write grid-layout, numbering, or interlock logic - CrosswordGenerator builds the numbered grid by interlocking words from CROSSWORD_BANK (425 words), and CrosswordBoard renders it and handles all input. Your whole job: import the 3 blocks, generate ONE puzzle from a daily seed, hand it to CrosswordBoard, and add the app-specific glue (localStorage save, Check/Reset). If your plan has a step about authoring puzzle data or grid algorithms, DELETE it.

FASTEST PATH (do this): the complete working build already exists at examples/crossword-mini/ (index.html + main.js + style.css, ~40 lines total). READ those three files and adapt them - keep the imports EXACTLY (./web-components/entities/CrosswordGenerator.js, ./web-components/resources/CrosswordDictionary.js, ./web-components/ui/CrosswordBoard.js), keep the generate-then-play structure, add the `mchatai-web-components-used` marker, and output ONE index.html. Do NOT re-derive the assembly across many steps - that times out. Per wisdom u-stop-reading-after-example, read at most those 3 files.
Build a recognizable compact crossword: numbered square grid, black '#' cells, Across/Down clue lists, selected-cell/word highlighting, keyboard entry, timer, score, persistent best time.

USE THE DEDICATED BLOCKS - do NOT hand-author a grid or hardcode puzzle data. Import the bank + generator + board: `import { CROSSWORD_BANK } from "./web-components/resources/CrosswordDictionary.js"; import { CrosswordGenerator } from "./web-components/entities/CrosswordGenerator.js"; import { CrosswordBoard } from "./web-components/ui/CrosswordBoard.js";`

GENERATE THEN PLAY: `const puzzle = new CrosswordGenerator({ size: 9, maxWords: 14, seed: daySeed }).generate(CROSSWORD_BANK); new CrosswordBoard({ root: document.getElementById('app'), puzzle, title: 'Mini Crossword', onSolved: ({seconds}) => {/* save best */} }).start();`

DAILY PUZZLE: derive a stable seed per day, e.g. `const daySeed = Math.floor(Date.now() / 86400000);` so everyone gets the same puzzle that day and Prev/Next can offset the seed. Same seed + same bank => identical puzzle (deterministic), which also makes golden-replay QA stable.

wisdom pg-016 is satisfied automatically: every answer comes from the dictionary, so every across AND down slot is a real word - the generator never pads or invents letters. wisdom pg-017 (>=100 puzzles) is satisfied: 425-word bank + seeded generation = effectively unlimited fresh valid puzzles.

MINI vs MIDI: size 5-7 + maxWords ~8-10 for an NYT-Mini feel; size 9-11 + maxWords ~14-18 for a fuller daily. The generator returns the trimmed bounding box (rows/cols), so render `puzzle.rows x puzzle.cols`.
Style the board's .cw-grid / .cw-cell / .cw-block / .cw-clue classes; resources.swatches gives a cohesive palette and resources.audio-manager adds key/solve sounds (both optional).

compositionRecipes.name: Quest Rooms
compositionRecipes.cloneTarget: Top-down room-maze adventure with keys, dragons, treasures, and locked gates
assemblyNotes: Build a recognizable Atari Adventure clone: room-to-room map, player avatar, keys, locked gates, treasure goal, simple enemies, inventory status, score/time, and reset.

Render the player as KNIGHT, the dragon as DRAGON, the treasure as TREASURE, the key as KEY, the locked gate as GATE - all bundled in resources/SpritePresets.js. Do NOT use plain colored rectangles or triangles for entities (wisdom rule vq-001).

Aim for at least 6-8 distinct rooms, not 3. Each room can mix terrain (forest, cavern, keep, vault, bridge, river) by reskinning the bg color and item layout. The lego-style draw layer makes adding rooms cheap.
Until dedicated room graph and inventory blocks are promoted, model rooms and items as small data tables in glue code.
Instantiate documented component constructors directly; there is no framework-level initialize() API.

compositionRecipes.name: Add Hosted Leaderboard (addon)
assemblyNotes: This is an ADDON recipe. It LAYERS ON TOP of the base game recipe (Snake, Pong, Tetris, etc.) - never replaces it. The marker MUST be `{recipe: <base-recipe-id>, addons: ["recipe.with-leaderboard"], components: [...all base + addon components...]}`.

IMPORT SHAPE - these are NAMED exports, not default exports. Use exactly: `import { GameOverWithRank } from "./web-components/ui/GameOverWithRank.js";` and `import { Leaderboard } from "./web-components/ui/Leaderboard.js";`. NEVER use `import GameOverWithRank from ...` (default-import form) - it resolves to `undefined` at runtime and the game-over screen will throw TypeError. The path prefix depends on where the importing file lives: from the artifact's `index.html` use `./web-components/ui/...`; from a sibling component file use `../../ui/...`.

MODE PREFERENCE - when generating a self-contained mini-app that should run at both /u/{slug}/ (browser, with bridge) AND file:// (mChatAI+ WKWebView preview, no bridge), prefer `mode: 'single-file-inline'` in the marker and inline the component source bodies with `BEGIN mChatAI Web Component: <id>` / `END` fence markers. The `module-imports` mode requires either the host page bridge or a properly-resolved relative path; at file:// the import will fail with CORS or a 404 and the game-over panel won't render. The Leaderboard component IS no-op-safe (returns null when bridge absent) so the import-failure mode is the only crash vector here - inline avoids it entirely.

ON GAME OVER (and ONLY on game-over - see u-018 + score-show-personal-best-on-game-over), call: `await GameOverWithRank.show({ host, score: finalScore, metadata: { level, moves, duration_ms }, onRestart: () => game.restart() });` This single call submits the score, renders the rank card OR a 'Sign in at mchatai.com' CTA (when not signed in), renders the top-10, and mounts a Play Again button.

Do NOT wrap `GameOverWithRank.show()` in a try/catch with a 'Leaderboard Unavailable' fallback panel. The component is no-op-safe - `submitFinal` returns null gracefully when the bridge is absent and the component handles that path internally (renders the sign-in CTA). A try/catch with a fallback adds dead code AND swallows real bugs (e.g. a wrong import shape that resolves to undefined) by routing them into the fallback. Just call it and trust the contract.

Do NOT call `Leaderboard.submitFinal` mid-game. Do NOT show rank or top-list while gameplay is active - that violates wisdom rule score-show-personal-best-on-game-over and u-hud-minimalism-meaningful-only.

Do NOT manually fetch /api/leaderboards/* or any HTTP endpoint. ALWAYS go through the Leaderboard / GameOverWithRank components. They handle bridge availability, sign-in state, network errors, and offline fallback transparently.

When `Leaderboard.submitFinal` resolves null (player not signed in OR bridge absent OR offline), the component renders a persuasive 'Sign in at mchatai.com to post your score' CTA. This is BY DESIGN - the leaderboard is the reason to create a free mChatAI account. Do not suppress the CTA or replace it with a generic 'leaderboard unavailable' message.
The board-id is supplied by the host page (window.mChatAI.leaderboard bridge) - the generated game never sets it. v1 -> v2 of the same published project share the same board.
Mobile-first: GameOverWithRank.show() is a single panel that fits 360px wide; do not stack multiple custom panels around it.

compositionRecipes.name: Add Sound Effects (addon)
assemblyNotes: This is an ADDON recipe. LAYERS ON TOP of the base game recipe - never replaces it. The marker MUST be `{recipe: <base-recipe-id>, addons: ["recipe.with-sound-effects"], components: [...all base + addon components...]}`.

IMPORT SHAPE - named exports only. From the artifact's `index.html`: `import { AudioManager } from "./web-components/resources/AudioManager.js";` and `import { MuteToggle } from "./web-components/ui/MuteToggle.js";`. From a sibling component: `../resources/AudioManager.js` and `./MuteToggle.js`.

USER-GESTURE GATE - iOS Safari requires a user gesture before the first AudioContext.resume(). DO NOT call `audio.sfx(...)` at module-load or before any tap/key. Instantiate the AudioManager AT module level (cheap, doesn't open the context), but defer the first sfx call to AFTER game-start (tap-to-start, first key press, mute-toggle click).
MOUNT THE TOGGLE - `MuteToggle.mount(document.body, { audio })` once per page. The toggle persists the mute state to localStorage; on next reload the player's choice is restored automatically.

WIRE THE COMMON EVENTS - on score increment: `audio.sfx('score')`. On collision/death/hit: `audio.sfx('hit')`. On game over: `audio.sfx('gameover')`. On level up: `audio.sfx('levelup')`. On menu/button taps if you want feedback: `audio.sfx('click')`. Pass unknown names safely - the dispatcher falls through to a soft default tone.

DO NOT layer on top of existing audio. If the base game already uses `new Audio(...)`, `AudioContext`, or has its own `.play()` calls, STOP and report 'already-sonified' to the user instead of double-wiring. The LLM judge filters most of these via the llmAppliesWhenPrompt criterion in offer.add-sound-effects but the assembly step is the last line of defense.
MOBILE-FIRST - the mute pill is 34x34px and floats top-right. Don't move it lower on mobile (it must not interfere with safe-area game HUD).

compositionRecipes.name: Todo List App
compositionRecipes.cloneTarget: Todoist-style task list with add/check/delete, filter All/Active/Done, and persistent storage
assemblyNotes: Layout: AppShell with title 'Tasks', the add-task TextInput in the header slot, the task list as <ul> of ListItem rows in the main slot, and EmptyState shown when zero tasks.
Add flow: TextInput with onSubmit handler - receives the typed value, push to state, clear the input, focus stays in input for rapid entry. NEVER lose focus on submit.
Each task row: ListItem with a Checkbox in the leading slot, primary text = task title, optional secondary = due date, trailing = a small ghost Button with x to delete.
Toggle done: Checkbox.onChange flips a `done` flag on the task. The Checkbox's built-in strikeOnCheck handles the line-through visual - do not add your own.
Filter tabs (All / Active / Done): three Button instances with variant='ghost' that swap to variant='primary' when active. Render the filtered list each click.

Counter: show 'N tasks left' below the list using a plain

with --mchat-text color. Update on every state mutation.
Persistence: store the array in localStorage under key 'taskflow.tasks'. Load on init, write on every mutation. Use window.mChatAI.storage if available, else localStorage.
EmptyState: title 'No tasks yet', subtitle 'Type above and press Enter to add your first task.', no CTA (the input is the CTA).
Do NOT build a custom drag-and-drop reordering UI in v1 - keep order = insertion order. Reorder is a v2 feature, premature in the foundation pass.
Mobile: full-bleed AppShell, sidebar collapses automatically via the built-in 720px breakpoint.

compositionRecipes.name: Notes App
compositionRecipes.cloneTarget: Bear/Apple-Notes-style note app with sidebar list + editor pane and search
assemblyNotes: Layout: AppShell with the note list as a <ul> of ListItem in the sidebarSlot, a TextInput at the top of mainSlot for the note title, and a <textarea> below it for the body.
Search: TextInput with placeholder 'Search notes...' mounted in the header slot. onInput filters the visible ListItem rows by title or body match (case-insensitive).
Sidebar rows: ListItem with primary=title, secondary=first 60 chars of body, onClick selects the note. Set active=true on the selected row, false on others.
Add note: a primary Button '+ New Note' in the sidebar header. Creates {id, title:'', body:'', createdAt: Date.now()}, selects it, focuses the title input.
Save: debounce 300ms on every keystroke in title or body, write the full notes array to localStorage under 'quicknotes.notes'.
Empty pane: when there are zero notes, show EmptyState in mainSlot with title 'No notes yet' and CTA 'Add your first note' that triggers the same handler as the + button.

When the user selects a note, render its title + body into the inputs. When they edit, update the in-memory note and re-render only the affected sidebar row's primary/secondary (do not re-render the whole list, breaks input focus).
Delete: a small ghost Button with (delete) in mainSlot top-right. Confirm with `if (confirm('Delete this note?'))` (skip a modal in v1 - Modal exists for future use).

compositionRecipes.name: Bookmark Manager
compositionRecipes.cloneTarget: Raindrop-style bookmark catalog with grid cards, tags, and add-via-modal
assemblyNotes: Layout: AppShell with tag list as ListItem rows in sidebarSlot, bookmark grid in mainSlot. Header slot holds a search TextInput and a primary Button '+ Add Bookmark'.

Bookmark card: render each bookmark as ListItem with leading=favicon (use https://www.google.com/s2/favicons?domain=<host>&sz=32), primary=title, secondary=hostname, trailing=ghost Button x delete. Use multiline=true so titles wrap.

Add flow: + Add Bookmark opens a Modal titled 'Add bookmark'. Body has 3 stacked TextInputs (label, URL, tags comma-separated) and a save primary Button in footerSlot. On save, parse host from URL, push {id, title, url, host, tags:[], createdAt}, close modal, clear inputs.
Tag sidebar: ListItem rows with leading=- and trailing=count. Click filters mainSlot to bookmarks containing that tag. Use a synthetic 'All' row at top.
Search: TextInput in header slot, onInput filters the visible bookmark grid by title or host or tag (case-insensitive substring).
Empty: EmptyState in mainSlot when zero bookmarks. Title 'No bookmarks yet', subtitle 'Click + Add Bookmark to save your first link', CTA reuses the modal opener.
Persistence: localStorage 'bookshelf.bookmarks' (array). Load on init, write on every mutation.

compositionRecipes.name: Focus Timer
compositionRecipes.cloneTarget: 25/5/15 minute focus-and-break timer with big circular countdown, controls, and session history
compositionRecipes.promptName: Focus timer with work and break cycles
assemblyNotes: Layout: centered single-column. Big TimerRing (size 280) at top, three Button controls below (Start, Pause, Reset), then a session history list as <ul> of ListItem rows. No AppShell - this is a single-purpose tool.
Modes: cycle through Focus(25min) -> ShortBreak(5min) -> Focus -> Focus -> Focus -> LongBreak(15min) -> repeat. Show the current mode as TimerRing's caption ('FOCUS', 'BREAK', 'LONG BREAK').

Auto-advance: when TimerRing.onComplete fires, push the completed session to history, switch to the next mode, reset the ring with the new total, do NOT auto-start (let user press Start - surprise-start is a known anti-pattern).
Controls: Start=primary, Pause=secondary, Reset=ghost. Toggle Start<->Pause labels based on ring.isRunning(). Reset stops the ring and rewinds to the current mode's total.
Session history: each completed focus session appends a ListItem with primary='Focus - 25:00', secondary=time-of-day (e.g. '2:14 PM'), leading=tomato emoji. Cap at 12 most-recent entries.
Empty history: EmptyState 'No sessions yet' / 'Press Start to begin your first 25-minute focus.', no CTA (the Start button is the CTA).

Notifications: if window.Notification && Notification.permission === 'granted', fire Notification('Focus complete') on each completion. Request permission lazily on first Start click, not on page load.
Audio cue: a short beep (Web Audio API oscillator, 880Hz for 200ms) on session complete. Respect a muted state in localStorage 'tomato.muted'.
Persistence: store today's session count in localStorage 'tomato.today.YYYY-MM-DD'. Show 'N completed today' under the ring.

compositionRecipes.name: Kanban Board
compositionRecipes.cloneTarget: Trello-style kanban board with columns, draggable cards, and detail modal
assemblyNotes: Layout: AppShell with the board area in mainSlot. The board is a horizontal flex row of Column elements that scrolls horizontally on mobile.
Each Column has title + accent color + count + a + Add Card footer. Set onAdd to open the add-card flow (inline TextInput or Modal - prefer inline for speed).
Cards: Card with title (task name), subtitle (assignee/due date), body (description), tags (array of label strings). onClick opens a detail Modal.
Detail Modal: editable title TextInput, description textarea, status select (which column to move to), Save + Delete buttons. Persist on Save.
Move card between columns: in v1, use a 'Move to ->' button on the card that cycles to next column. Drag-drop is a v2 feature; do NOT attempt HTML5 drag-drop here (it's brittle on iOS Safari).
Default columns: Backlog, To Do, In Progress, Done. Let users add/remove columns in v2 - v1 ships with these 4 fixed.
Persistence: localStorage 'tasklanes.board' = {columns: [...], cards: [...]}. Load on init, write on every mutation.
Empty column: EmptyState inside the column bodySlot when zero cards. Subtitle: 'No cards yet - click + Add Card'.
Mobile: AppShell handles sidebar collapse; the column row scrolls horizontally with momentum (touch-action: pan-x).

compositionRecipes.name: Expense Tracker
compositionRecipes.cloneTarget: Mint-style expense logger with form, sortable table, and category donut chart
assemblyNotes: Layout: AppShell title 'Spendwise', monthly summary at top of mainSlot (DonutChart on left + 'this month' KPI text on right), then the DataTable of expenses below.

Add flow: + Add Expense Button (primary) in header slot opens a Modal with 4 stacked TextInputs: date (type='date'), category (select-from-existing or new), amount (type='number'), note. Save button persists and closes.
DataTable columns: date (sortable), category (renders as TagChip with category color), amount (right-aligned, sortable, formatted as $X.XX), note (truncated), trailing x delete button.
DonutChart: data computed from the current month's expenses grouped by category. centerLabel = $TOTAL.XX, centerSubtitle = 'this month'. Use TagChip color for slice color (consistent palette).
Category colors: deterministic from category name (hash -> palette index). Pass the same color to TagChip and DonutChart so they match visually.
Month switcher: prev/next month buttons in header slot; default = current month. State key 'spendwise.viewMonth' = 'YYYY-MM'.
Persistence: localStorage 'spendwise.expenses' = array of {id, date, category, amount, note}.
Empty: EmptyState in mainSlot when zero expenses this month, with CTA 'Add expense' that opens the Modal.

compositionRecipes.name: Stellar Defender
compositionRecipes.cloneTarget: Fixed-shooter arcade: rows of descending aliens march left-right and step down, player ship moves left-right at the bottom firing single shots upward, destructible bunker shields, occasional UFO bonus across the top.
assemblyNotes: Layout: portrait/landscape arcade play area. Top to bottom: mini-header, alien formation, bunker row, player ship, bottom HUD with score/lives/wave.

Alien formation: 5 rows x 11 columns by default. The whole formation moves as a single block - horizontal step right until rightmost column hits the wall, then descend one row and reverse direction. Step interval shortens as alien count drops (the iconic march speedup).

Each alien is its own Entity instance using entities.entity. Different point values per row (top=30, middle=20, bottom=10). When destroyed, remove from formation grid (mark cell empty). Wave ends when all aliens are destroyed.

Player ship: bottom row, horizontal-only movement (left/right arrows or A/D), constrained to screen width. Fires one projectile upward at a time - cannot fire again until the previous shot leaves the screen or hits something.

Alien bullets: aliens at the bottom of each column periodically fire downward. Use the column-bottom rule (only the lowest-still-alive alien in each column can fire). Limit concurrent alien bullets to 3-5 to keep the game playable.

Bunker shields: 3-4 destructible bunkers between aliens and player. Each bunker is a small grid of blocks (e.g., 8x6 micro-cells). Both player and alien bullets chip away at adjacent blocks on impact. Bunkers do NOT regenerate between waves (classic rule).

UFO bonus: at random intervals (every 20-30s), a UFO sprite traverses the top of the screen horizontally. Hitting it awards a random bonus (50/100/150/300 pts). UFO exits if not hit before reaching the opposite edge.
Lose conditions: (a) all 3 player lives consumed by alien bullets, OR (b) any alien reaches the bottom row of the play area. Trigger ui.restart-overlay on game-over.
Wave progression: when all aliens cleared, start next wave with formation reset to top, faster initial step interval. Bunker damage persists.
Sounds (optional via resources.audio-manager): 4-tone descending march loop (pitch drops as aliens die), player shot, alien hit, UFO whoop, player death.

DO NOT reuse entities.asteroid or recipe.arcade-top-down (Asteroids) as a base - the genres are fundamentally different. Space Invaders has FORMATION movement, FIXED-position player, DESTRUCTIBLE bunkers. Asteroids has FREE-flying ship with momentum, omnidirectional play, no bunkers.

compositionRecipes.name: All-Fives Dominoes
compositionRecipes.cloneTarget: All-Fives (Muggins) dominoes: 4-player double-six tile game where doubles act as spinners that allow perpendicular branches, and a player scores points equal to the sum of the open end pips whenever that sum is a multiple of 5 after their play.
assemblyNotes: Game variant: ALL-FIVES (also called Muggins). This is the most common scored dominoes variant. DO NOT default to plain block-and-draw with end-of-round pip-counting - that is a different variant and produces unfair scoring for casual players who learned All-Fives.

Layout: 4-seat table. YOU at bottom (hand fanned face-up), then West (left, vertical face-down), North (top, horizontal face-down), East (right, vertical face-down). Center is the play area where the chain extends. Use ui.scoreboard for the running points-to-121 per player.
Tile set: double-six (0:0 through 6:6 = 28 tiles total). Shuffle, deal 7 tiles to each player, remaining 0 tiles form the boneyard for draw-dominoes rules.
Starting player: holder of highest double leads with that double. The first double played BECOMES the spinner. Subsequent doubles are NOT spinners (only the first one).

Spinner mechanics (CRITICAL - this is the All-Fives rule the most common LLM-generated dominoes apps get wrong): the spinner double can have FOUR connections (one on each of its 4 sides). After the spinner is played, the next two ends are along the line (left/right of the spinner). After both line-ends are taken, perpendicular branches (above/below the spinner) become legal. Track all open ends as an array, not just leftEnd/rightEnd.

Open-ends state: maintain a list of currently exposed (uncovered) pip-values. For the first tile (a double, the spinner), all 4 sides are open initially - but only TWO sides count toward the open-end pip sum until perpendicular branches start (per All-Fives convention, exposed-pip-sum uses the unique value on each open end of the chain; for a double spinner with nothing attached yet, the open ends are just the double's two equal values = 12 if 6:6, but conventionally we count it as 12 since both ends are exposed).

All-Fives scoring (CRITICAL): after EVERY tile placement, recompute the sum of pips on all currently-open ends of the chain. If that sum is divisible by 5 AND greater than 0, award those points to the player who just played. Example: 6:6 spinner is played first (open ends = 6 + 6 = 12, no score). Player 2 places 6:3 perpendicular on one of the spinner's free sides, the new open ends are now [6 (other line end), 6 (other free spinner side), 3 (the new tile's free half)] = 6+6+3 = 15. 15 % 5 = 0, so player 2 scores 15 points.

Note that placing along the line (train-style) on the 6:6 spinner only exposes one new value: 6:3 placed train-style on a 6-end would leave open ends [6 (other line end), 3 (this tile's free half)] = 9. 9 is NOT divisible by 5 - no score. The perpendicular play is the strategic move. The game MUST allow the player to choose between line and perpendicular placements when both are legal.

When a double (non-spinner) is played, it sits perpendicular to the chain line (across the chain) and exposes only ONE end-value (its single value) for All-Fives scoring purposes, since both halves show the same number.

Player turn UI: highlight the active player's seat. The human's hand shows playable tiles brighter than unplayable ones. Clicking a playable tile shows an arrow on each legal open end - clicking one of those arrows places the tile there. If no tile is playable, the human must Draw from the boneyard (or Pass if the boneyard is empty).

CPU strategy (the bug C8 hit): when evaluating moves, the CPU MUST consider perpendicular placements on the spinner, not just line placements. A naive 'place on left/right end' CPU misses the spinner branches entirely. Use a small move-scoring function: for each legal placement (line + perpendicular), compute the resulting open-end sum, prefer placements that score (sum % 5 == 0), then break ties by 'plays high pips first' to dump big tiles.

Round end: a player wins the round when they play their last tile (or, if the game is blocked because no one can play, lowest remaining-pip-count wins). Award them the rounded-down-to-nearest-5 sum of opponents' remaining pips. First to 121 wins the game.
Restart: use ui.restart-overlay for game-over (someone reached 121) with the final scoreboard.

compositionRecipes.name: Multi-Timer (Kitchen Timer)
compositionRecipes.cloneTarget: Kitchen-timer mini-app: add any number of named countdown timers (eg 'pasta 8min', 'eggs 6min'), all tick down simultaneously on one screen, each plays a distinct chime when it completes.
assemblyNotes: Layout: vertical single-column. Mini-header at top with title 'Kitchen Timer'. Below: a 'Add timer' row with a TextInput (label + duration) + Button. Below that: a stack of active timer cards, each rendering a small TimerRing + label + Cancel button.
Each timer is INDEPENDENT - uses its own setInterval / requestAnimationFrame ticker. Multiple timers count down simultaneously on the same screen. Show remaining time as MM:SS.
Adding a timer: parse the input like 'pasta 8min' or 'eggs 6:30' or '90s'. Accept formats: 'NAME Hh MMm SSs', 'NAME M:SS', 'NAME Ss'. Default to MM:SS if only a number is entered.

Each timer renders one ui.timer-ring instance (size 80-120px) with the timer label as the ring's caption. Set totalSeconds + remaining at construction. Use the ring's onComplete callback to play the chime and mark the timer done.

Chime: each timer plays a DIFFERENT chime when done so you can tell them apart by sound. Use AudioManager with 3-5 short beep tones at different pitches (e.g., 440, 523, 659, 784, 988 Hz). Round-robin assign pitch by timer index.

Persistence (localStorage): save active timers' {label, totalSeconds, startedAt}. On page load, restore them with adjusted remaining = totalSeconds - (now - startedAt). Drop expired ones from storage but show a 'completed' chip for 30s after page load so the user knows what just finished.
Layout for many timers: when there are 6+ active timers, switch to a 2-column grid. Visual feedback: timer ring color shifts to --mchat-success-warm in the last 10% of remaining time.
Cancel button: each timer card has a small X to remove it before it completes. Confirm with a quick toast 'Timer cancelled'.

DO NOT route this to recipe.pomodoro-timer. That recipe is for a SINGLE 25/5/15 Pomodoro cycle, not a multi-timer kitchen app. The classifier mismatch was a 2026-05-19 QAFlywheel regression - kitchen timer requires concurrent timers with custom durations + labels.
Restart: 'Clear all' button at the bottom resets everything. Use ui.restart-overlay only if every timer has completed (game-over-style screen).

compositionRecipes.name: Decision Maker (Weighted Spin Wheel)
compositionRecipes.cloneTarget: Decision-maker mini-app: user pastes a list of options, optionally weights each (1-10), then spins a colorful wheel that lands on one option weighted-randomly. Keeps a short history of recent decisions with timestamps.
assemblyNotes: Layout: AppShell-like with title at top, a config panel on the left (or above on mobile) holding the option list, and the spin wheel on the right (or below). Mobile: stack vertically - wheel on top, options below.

Options input: a TextInput where the user pastes a multi-line list. Each line becomes one option. Parse 'NAME' or 'NAME * WEIGHT' (weight 1-10, default 1). Show each parsed option as a ListItem row with a 1-10 weight slider.

Wheel rendering: HTML5 canvas, ~320x320 px. Divide into sectors proportional to weights (sector angle = 2*Math.PI * weight / totalWeight). Each sector gets a distinct color from swatch.accentRing palette (cycle through 8-12 colors). Sector label drawn radially inside the sector.

Spinning: animate canvas rotation with requestAnimationFrame for ~3-4 seconds, easing out cubically. Final rotation is computed by picking a weighted-random index FIRST, then rotating to land on that index's sector center (plus 4-6 full rotations for the spin effect). The wheel MUST be deterministic given the chosen index - no 'random stop' that could land on a different sector.
Pointer: a fixed triangular pointer at the top of the wheel indicating the winning sector. Highlight winning sector with a glow effect after stop.
Result display: large text below the wheel showing '-> <winning option>'. Add a 'Spin again' button.
History: keep last 5 decisions with timestamp (HH:MM). Render as a small ListItem stack below the result. Persist in localStorage under 'decision-maker.history'.
Edge cases: empty option list disables Spin. Single option = it always wins (still allow spin for visual fun). Weights summing to 0 = uniform random.
Pure Math.random for the weighted-random pick. Use the standard 'cumulative weight + random in [0, totalWeight]' technique.
Visual style: bright, playful, slightly arcade-like. Sector colors should be high-contrast against white sector labels.

compositionRecipes.name: Timezone Meeting Finder
compositionRecipes.cloneTarget: Cross-timezone meeting-time finder: user adds attendee cities, the app shows a horizontal time-band (6am-10pm) per city, and highlights the overlap windows when everyone is in working hours (eg 9am-5pm local).
assemblyNotes: Layout: AppShell with title 'Meeting Time Finder'. Top input row: TextInput for city name (with autocomplete from a short hard-coded list of ~40 common cities + their IANA timezone strings) + Add button. Below: a vertical stack of attendee rows, each showing the city name + a horizontal time-band visualization.

Time-band: each city's row has a horizontal bar from 6am to 10pm LOCAL time. The bar uses CSS grid (16 cells, one per hour). Working hours (9am-5pm) are colored darker. Outside working hours but within 6-10pm is lighter.

Reference time: the topmost row is the user's local time. All other cities show their LOCAL time corresponding to the same UTC moment. Use Intl.DateTimeFormat with the city's timezone to compute local hour offset.

Overlap highlight: as the user drags a time slider (or scrolls the bands), the moment is shown as a vertical line across all rows. If that moment is within working hours for EVERY attendee, all bars glow with a green tint and a 'OK for everyone' badge appears above. If any city is outside, those rows tint red.

City list: hard-coded array of about 40 well-known cities {name, tz, lat?}. Examples: 'San Francisco' (America/Los_Angeles), 'New York' (America/New_York), 'London' (Europe/London), 'Berlin' (Europe/Berlin), 'Tokyo' (Asia/Tokyo), 'Sydney' (Australia/Sydney), 'Dubai' (Asia/Dubai), etc. Include 4-5 cities from each major continent.
Daylight saving: rely on Intl.DateTimeFormat for the actual offset on the chosen date. Don't hard-code offsets.
Persistence: save the attendee list in localStorage as an array of {name, tz}. Restore on page load.
Mobile layout: time-bands shrink to fit screen width (use min-width:8px per cell). The slider becomes a horizontal swipe gesture.

Edge cases: only 1 attendee = always-overlap. Empty list = show empty-state with example ('Add Bob in San Francisco, then Alice in Berlin to see when you both work'). All-night times outside 6-10pm rendered with a moon icon as a visual cue.

compositionRecipes.name: Phonetic Alphabet Trainer
compositionRecipes.cloneTarget: NATO phonetic alphabet trainer: shows a letter, user types or selects the correct phonetic word (Alpha, Bravo, Charlie, ...). Tracks accuracy and per-letter weak spots.
assemblyNotes: Quiz set: hard-code the 26 NATO phonetic words. {A: Alpha, B: Bravo, C: Charlie, D: Delta, E: Echo, F: Foxtrot, G: Golf, H: Hotel, I: India, J: Juliett, K: Kilo, L: Lima, M: Mike, N: November, O: Oscar, P: Papa, Q: Quebec, R: Romeo, S: Sierra, T: Tango, U: Uniform, V: Victor, W: Whiskey, X: X-ray, Y: Yankee, Z: Zulu}.

Card flow: show one letter at a time, large and centered. User types the phonetic word in a TextInput OR taps from a 4-button multiple-choice (correct + 3 distractors). Mode toggle: 'Type' or 'Multiple Choice'.

Scoring: each correct answer is +1 point and the streak counter increases. Wrong answers reset the streak and add the letter to a 'weak letters' list. The scoreboard shows {score, streak, accuracy %, total answered}.

Weak letters: maintain a Set of letters where the user got it wrong recently. Bias the random letter picker to draw from weak letters 60% of the time, fresh letters 40% - so the trainer focuses on what the user struggles with.
Session: rounds of 20 letters, then a 'session summary' overlay using ui.restart-overlay showing accuracy, time, and weak letters. 'Start new session' restarts.
Audio: optional - play the spoken phonetic word on each card reveal (browser SpeechSynthesis API, voice='Daniel' or similar). Toggle in settings.
Persistence: save weak-letters set + lifetime accuracy stats in localStorage. On launch, show 'Pick up where you left off' with a 'Reset progress' button.
Visual style: clean, large fonts. Letter card is the dominant element on the screen. Use --mchat-accent for correct, --mchat-accentWarm-or-error for wrong.

compositionRecipes.name: Language Phrase Match
assemblyNotes: Build a memory match where each pair is a Spanish phrase and its English translation (NON-identical cards that share a hidden pair id). Use the deep bank: `import { SPANISH_PHRASES } from "./web-components/resources/SpanishPhrasebook.js"; import { MemoryMatchBoard } from "./web-components/entities/MemoryMatchBoard.js";`

DECK: pick N pairs from the bank and build a non-identical deck with the dedicated helper: `const pairs = shuffle(SPANISH_PHRASES).slice(0, rows*cols/2).map(p => ({ a: p.es, b: p.en })); board.setCards(MemoryMatchBoard.buildPairDeck(pairs, rows*cols));`. buildPairDeck makes the es card and en card share a `value` (so they match) but keep distinct `face` text (what you render). Do NOT use buildShuffledDeck here - that is for IDENTICAL pairs (emoji).

RENDER cell.face (the phrase text), not cell.value (the hidden pair id). Show '' or a back design when state==='down'; show cell.face when 'up'/'matched'. Match logic is identical to any memory game: flipCard(r,c) -> 'first'|'match'|'mismatch'; on 'mismatch' setTimeout(~900ms) then board.resolveMismatch().
SIZES: 4x4 (8 pairs) easy, 4x6 (12) medium, 6x6 (18) hard. Phrase tiles need more room than emoji - use a readable font-size, word-break, and >=44px touch targets (mob-002).

VARIANT - flashcards/quiz instead of match: feed the same bank to entities.content-dictionary as { prompt: p.es, answer: p.en } and draw/check/hint. The bank also carries `category` so you can theme a round (dining, directions, etc.).

The bank is the deep content (421 phrases, ~one per day of the year); swap SpanishPhrasebook for any other { es/en } or { term/definition } bank to reskin to another language or subject without touching the game logic.

absorptionProcess: Select a known-good mini-app from catalog/good-games.json or /admin/template-candidates.
Extract reusable mechanics into a focused ES module with a documented constructor and small method surface.
Reassemble the source game as an example using imports from this catalog.
Run module import checks and browser smoke tests.
Mark the component ready in this manifest only after the reassembled example behaves like the source game.
